title: pellaea ternifolia subsp. weddelliana
intro: Pellaea ternifolia subsp. weddelliana, also known as Weddell's button fern or Weddell's button fern subsp., is a subspecies of fern native to New Zealand, characterized by its dark green, leathery fronds. The plant has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, including its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Pellaea ternifolia subsp. weddelliana include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, or as a tincture, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
